memes.label: Aging
memes.description: The experience of growing old in His Family, embodied primarily through Roger Gale as he confronts the passage of time and his own mortality.

memes.label: "Allan Baird"
memes.description: "A doctor and social reformer, friend and colleague of Deborah Gale, who represents the progressive spirit of early 20th-century medicine and social conscience in *His Family*."

memes.label: Ambition
memes.description: The driving force behind characters' personal and professional aspirations in His Family, often creating tension between individual goals and family obligations.

memes.label: America
memes.description: America serves as both the national setting and a thematic touchstone in "His Family," representing the country of immigration, opportunity, and changing social values that shape the Gale family's story.

memes.label: Beauty
memes.description: The quality of radiant attractiveness in His Family, most embodied by the younger generation, that elicits both admiration and a sense of passing time.

memes.label: "Betsy Sidney"
memes.description: "Edith Sidney's daughter — one of the 'small ones' whose childhood represents the next generation of the Gale family and the continuity Roger Gale most desires in *His Family*."

memes.label: Bridgeport
memes.description: Bridgeport, Connecticut, is referenced in "His Family" as the home of a wealthy widowed aunt connected to the Sidney family, representing the network of family connections extending beyond New York City.

memes.label: Broadway
memes.description: Broadway, the iconic New York City thoroughfare, appears in Ernest Poole's novel His Family as a symbol of urban spectacle, modernity, and the cultural transformation of early 20th-century America.

memes.label: "Bruce"
memes.description: "A young architect, war veteran, and Deborah Gale's unconventional love interest — a builder of the new city who is also broken by modern warfare in *His Family*."

memes.label: "Bruce and Deborah's Courtship"
memes.description: "The unconventional courtship between Deborah Gale and Bruce — a relationship that honors love without demanding marriage in *His Family*."

memes.label: Business
memes.description: Business is both Roger Gale's professional domain and a key thematic concern in "His Family," representing the forces of commerce, enterprise, and economic change that shape early twentieth-century New York.

memes.label: Change
memes.description: The pervasive force of transformation in His Family, felt in the city, the family, and individual lives as the old order gives way to the new.

memes.label: Childhood
memes.description: Childhood as a recurring lens through which Roger Gale understands his own past, his children's lives, and the continuity of the family across generations in His Family.

memes.label: Community
memes.description: The web of shared life and mutual obligation in His Family, from the tenement neighborhoods to Deborah's school community.

memes.label: Concepts of Modernity
memes.description: Thematic concepts in His Family tagged with "modernity" — the forces of change reshaping early 20th-century American life.

memes.label: Cooper Union
memes.description: Cooper Union, a landmark educational institution and meeting hall in New York City, serves as a site for public speeches, political gatherings, and social reform meetings in His Family.
aliases: Cooper Union for the Advancement of Science and Art

memes.label: Courage
memes.description: The quiet resolve characters in *His Family* draw upon to face difficult conversations, life changes, and personal challenges — a domestic rather than heroic virtue.

memes.label: Dave Royce
memes.description: The longtime farmer of Roger Gale's New Hampshire farm, a cautious and experienced voice of rural wisdom.

memes.label: "Death"
memes.description: "The recurring agent of change in His Family — death punctuates the novel at every major turn, from Judith Gale's early passing to the wartime losses that reshape Roger Gale's understanding of family, duty, and legacy."

memes.label: "Deborah and Roger's Reconciliation"
memes.description: "The gradual reconciliation between Roger Gale and his daughter Deborah — the emotional climax of *His Family* in which Roger finally understands and accepts the life Deborah has built."

memes.label: "Deborah Gale"
memes.description: "The middle daughter of Roger Gale — a reformer who rejects traditional marriage to found a school for immigrant children in the tenement districts, redefining family as public service in early 20th-century New York."

memes.label: "Deborah's 'Family of Her Own'"
memes.description: "Deborah Gale's declaration that she has built 'a family of my own' through her tenement school — the philosophical event that redefines the novel's central concept of family in *His Family*."

memes.label: "Deborah's School"
memes.description: "The network of progressive schools Deborah Gale builds in New York's immigrant tenement districts — the alternative 'family of her own' that redefines what family, service, and a woman's life can mean in *His Family*."

memes.label: "Disease"
memes.description: "Disease as literal affliction and social metaphor — from Pott's disease and tuberculosis to the moral sickness of modern urban life — in Ernest Poole's *His Family*."

memes.label: Doctor
memes.description: The figure of the doctor in His Family — Bruce's professional identity, the cultural authority of medicine, and the tension between professional confidence and the limits of healing.

memes.label: Doctor Lake
memes.description: A prominent child specialist in New York who has cared for Edith Sidney's children for years.

memes.label: "Downtown New York"
memes.description: "The commercial heart of New York City — where Roger Gale built his business and where the 'tumultuous modern town' rises most aggressively, embodying the economic forces transforming *His Family*'s world."

memes.label: Dream
memes.description: The role of dreams and dreaming in His Family — youthful aspirations, romantic ideals, and the tension between what is imagined and what life actually delivers.

memes.label: "Duty"
memes.description: "The moral obligation — felt most acutely by Roger Gale — to act in the interests of family, tradition, and conscience, even at the cost of personal freedom, in Ernest Poole's *His Family*."

memes.label: "Edith Sidney"
memes.description: "The eldest Gale daughter — a practical, domestic woman whose marriage and five children represent the traditional path of family continuity in *His Family*."

memes.label: "The Birth of Edith's Children"
memes.description: "The births of Edith Sidney's five children — the events that bring 'new lives in his family' and represent the continuity Roger Gale most values in *His Family*."

memes.label: "Education"
memes.description: "The transformative power of education — from Deborah Gale's progressive schools in the immigrant quarters to the broader question of how the next generation should be taught and raised in *His Family*."

memes.label: Elizabeth Sidney
memes.description: Elizabeth Sidney is the daughter of Edith and George Sidney, representing the second generation of the Gale family in Ernest Poole's "His Family."

memes.label: "Europe"
memes.description: "The distant world of European travel and war that pulls Laura and Harold away from the Gale family — representing both the allure of cosmopolitan escape and the devastation of modern conflict in *His Family*."

memes.label: European Places
memes.description: Places in Europe referenced in His Family — cities and countries that figure in the characters' travels and memories.

memes.label: "Family"
memes.description: "The central organizing principle of His Family — the relationships, obligations, and emotional bonds that both sustain and constrain Roger Gale and his three daughters across a generation of transformation in early 20th-century New York."

memes.label: Family Characters
memes.description: All characters in His Family tagged with the "family" theme — the Gale family and those drawn into its orbit.

memes.label: Family Themes
memes.description: Thematic concepts in His Family tagged with "family" — the bonds, duties, tensions, and legacies that define domestic life.

memes.label: Fear
memes.description: The anxieties that haunt the Gale family — fear of change, fear of loss, and the "fearful season" of World War I that transforms their world.

memes.label: "Forgiveness"
memes.description: "The act and theme of forgiveness between family members in His Family, often sought in moments of crisis and linked to the novel's exploration of familial duty."

memes.label: "Founding of Deborah's School"
memes.description: "Deborah Gale's establishment of a progressive school for immigrant children in New York's tenement district — the vocational event that redefines her life and the novel's vision of family in *His Family*."

memes.label: France
memes.description: France is a European nation referenced in "His Family" in connection with Deborah's youthful travels, George Sidney's charitable project, and the Great War that transforms the lives of all the characters.

memes.label: Freedom
memes.description: The ideal of personal freedom as embodied by Isadore Freedom, an immigrant who renamed himself after the concept, and as a source of tension between individual desires and family obligations.

memes.label: "Generational Change"
memes.description: "The widening gap between parents and children, old ways and new, that drives the central tension of *His Family* — a novel about what one generation passes to the next and what it can never pass."

memes.label: "George Sidney"
memes.description: "Edith Gale's husband — a steady, conventional man whose marriage and family life represent the domestic norm in *His Family*."

memes.label: Germany
memes.description: Germany appears in His Family primarily in the context of World War I, representing the distant conflict that increasingly impacts the lives of the Gale family in New York.

memes.label: Gossip
memes.description: The social currency of talk, rumor, and scandal that binds and divides the characters in His Family.

memes.label: "Grandchildren"
memes.description: "The role of grandchildren in His Family — particularly Roger Gale's grandson — as symbols of continuity, hope, and the transmission of values across generations."

memes.label: Grief
memes.description: The experience of loss and mourning in His Family, revealing how different characters respond to death and how grief reshapes family dynamics.

memes.label: "Hannah"
memes.description: "The devoted Irish nurse who serves the Gale-Sidney household — a 'devoted Irish girl' whose loyalty and presence bridge the working-class immigrant experience and the family's domestic life in *His Family*."

memes.label: "Harold Sloane"
memes.description: "A wealthy young man who marries Laura Gale, representing inherited privilege and the allure of social ascent in *His Family*."

memes.label: "Home"
memes.description: "The domestic spaces — both physical and emotional — that anchor Roger Gale and his family through decades of change in early 20th-century New York."

memes.label: Hope
memes.description: Hope is a recurring emotional and thematic thread in "His Family," representing the forward-looking impulse that sustains characters through loss, change, and the uncertainties of modernity.

memes.label: "Immigration"
memes.description: "The presence and experience of immigrants in early 20th-century New York as depicted in His Family — a background pressure that shapes the city and the lives of its established families."

memes.label: Isadore Freedom
memes.description: A young Russian-Polish immigrant and librarian who embodies the ideal of education as liberation, connected to Deborah Gale's school.

memes.label: "Italy"
memes.description: "A European destination referenced in *His Family* — representing the Old World's cultural wealth and the appeal of escape from the pressures of modern American life."

memes.label: Joe
memes.description: Joe is an Italian immigrant boy in Ernest Poole's "His Family" whose tragic story — a death sentence for murder — illustrates the harsh realities of New York's tenement districts and the limits of social reform.

memes.label: "Johnny Geer"
memes.description: "A crippled boy from Deborah Gale's tenement school who becomes a surrogate son to Roger Gale — embodying the novel's theme of how family can extend beyond blood in *His Family*."

memes.label: "Judith Gale"
memes.description: "Roger Gale's deceased wife — the absent center of *His Family* whose memory, values, and dying wish to 'keep the family together' shape every major character's life."

memes.label: "Judith Gale's Death"
memes.description: "The founding event of His Family — Judith Gale's premature death at age twenty-nine leaves Roger a widower with three small daughters, shaping the emotional architecture of the entire novel."

memes.label: "Judith Gale's Funeral"
memes.description: "The funeral of Judith Gale — the ritual of public mourning that marks the formal end of the Gale family's old life and the beginning of Roger's long widowhood in *His Family*."

memes.label: Kindness
memes.description: The moments of compassion and tenderness that punctuate the emotional landscape of His Family — fleeting, imperfect, but profoundly human.

memes.label: "Laura Sloane"
memes.description: "The middle Gale daughter — a beauty who marries into wealth and moves beyond the family orbit, representing the allure and cost of social ascent in *His Family*."

memes.label: "Laura's Departure for Europe"
memes.description: "Laura and Harold Sloane's departure for Europe — the event that most clearly marks Laura's separation from the Gale family in *His Family*."

memes.label: "Laura Gale's Engagement"
memes.description: "The courtship and engagement of Laura Gale to Harold Sloane — the romantic event that signals Laura's departure from the Gale family orbit in *His Family*."

memes.label: "Laura's Wedding"
memes.description: "The marriage of Laura Gale to Harold Sloane — a pivotal event that transforms the Gale home into a wedding display, exposes generational tensions, and marks Laura's departure from the family circle into the world of wealth and social ambition."

memes.label: Law
memes.description: The presence of law and legal institutions in His Family — from Allan's legal career to the broader question of what rules govern family relationships and social order.

memes.label: "List of Characters"
memes.description: "A dynamic index of all characters in *His Family* by Ernest Poole. Sorted by label for easy browsing."

memes.label: "List of Concepts"
memes.description: "A dynamic index of thematic concepts explored in *His Family* — family, modernity, change, duty, class, and more."

memes.label: "List of Events"
memes.description: "A dynamic index of key events in *His Family* — weddings, deaths, departures, and the turning points that shape the Gale family story."

memes.label: "List of Places"
memes.description: "A dynamic index of all places referenced in *His Family* — New York City neighborhoods, landmarks, and beyond."

memes.label: London
memes.description: London appears in His Family as a destination for international travel and as a point of reference during the wartime period of the novel.

memes.label: Loneliness
memes.description: A profound emotional state experienced by multiple characters in the novel, often amid — rather than apart from — their families.

memes.label: "Loss"
memes.description: "The many forms of loss — of faith, of loved ones, of youth, of certainty — that shape the emotional landscape of Ernest Poole's *His Family*."

memes.label: "Love and Courtship"
memes.description: "The varieties of romantic love — from Roger's deep partnership with Judith to the courtships of his daughters — as they shape and complicate family life in *His Family*."

memes.label: Madge Deering
memes.description: Edith Sidney's friend, a widowed mother of four daughters living in Morristown, New Jersey, who embodies resilience and modernity.

memes.label: "Marriage"
memes.description: "The institution of marriage as it shapes the lives and choices of the Gale family — from Roger's remembered union with Judith to the three daughters' different approaches to love, partnership, and independence in *His Family*."

memes.label: Medicine
memes.description: The role of medicine and medical care in His Family, from patent remedies to professional doctors, reflecting early 20th-century attitudes toward health and healing.

memes.label: Memory
memes.description: Memory as the thread connecting Roger Gale to his late wife Judith, to his own childhood, and to the family story he hopes will outlive him.

memes.label: "Modernity"
memes.description: "The forces of urbanization, technological progress, and changing social values that transform early 20th-century New York and challenge the traditional world of Roger Gale in *His Family*."

memes.label: Morristown
memes.description: Morristown, New Jersey, is where Madge Deering lives with her four daughters, representing a simpler, rural alternative to the bustle of New York City in "His Family."

memes.label: Most Referenced Pages
memes.description: Statistical ranking of the most-referenced pages in the His Family wiki, sorted by total backlink count.

memes.label: Motherhood
memes.description: Motherhood as a central and contested concept in the novel — Edith's traditional maternal devotion versus Deborah's vision of collective, institutionalized care for children.

memes.label: Mrs. Berry
memes.description: The keeper of a boarding house on the Lower East Side where Isadore Freedom and others connected to Deborah Gale's work reside.

memes.label: Mrs. Neale
memes.description: Mrs. Neale is a minor domestic character in "His Family" who works as a washerwoman in one of the Gale family households.

memes.label: Mrs. Raymond
memes.description: A neighbor of the Gale family in their New York apartment building, mentioned briefly in Chapter 3 of His Family. Mrs. Raymond represents the casual, semi-anonymous social connections of urban apartment living.

memes.label: Music
memes.description: Music as a cultural and emotional force that divides Roger's generation from his children's, most powerfully rendered in the Tschaikovsky symphony scene.

memes.label: Human Nature
memes.description: A recurring lens through which characters in *His Family* explain and accept the behavior of others — invoked as both explanation and resignation.

memes.label: Neighborhood
memes.description: The changing urban neighborhood as both a physical setting and a symbol of generational transformation in His Family.

memes.label: New England
memes.description: New England represents the ancestral homeland and cultural heritage of Roger Gale's family in "His Family," embodying the old-stock American values of thrift, reserve, and moral seriousness that contrast with the immigrant energy of New York City.

memes.label: "New Hampshire Farm"
memes.description: "Roger Gale's childhood home in the New Hampshire mountains — the rural origin that shaped his character and the summer retreat that represents an older, simpler America in *His Family*."

memes.label: "New York City"
memes.description: "The primary setting of His Family — a city in explosive transformation from the 1880s through the 1910s, whose growth mirrors and drives the dissolution of Roger Gale's traditional family structure."

memes.label: Nostalgia
memes.description: The persistent backward glance toward a lost past that shapes the emotional world of His Family.

memes.label: Parenthood
memes.description: The experience and obligations of parenthood in His Family, explored through Roger Gale's relationship with his daughters and his observations of tenement families.

memes.label: Paris
memes.description: Paris is a European city referenced in "His Family" as a destination for travel and cultural aspiration, particularly associated with Laura Sloane's cosmopolitan ambitions and Deborah's youthful European journeys.

memes.label: Peace
memes.description: Peace — both as domestic tranquility and spiritual acceptance — is the elusive goal toward which the characters of "His Family" strive, and which Roger Gale ultimately achieves in his final acceptance of change and loss.

memes.label: Poverty
memes.description: The economic hardship that shadows the novel's working-class characters and drives Deborah's commitment to educational reform in the immigrant districts of early 20th-century New York.

memes.label: Pride
memes.description: The quiet, often unspoken pride that Roger Gale takes in his family and home, and the wounded pride that complicates the lives of his children.

memes.label: Progress
memes.description: The novel's ambivalent treatment of social and personal advancement — progress is shown as both inevitable and destabilizing in Ernest Poole's *His Family*.

memes.label: "Reform"
memes.description: "The impulse toward social improvement — through education, through institutional change, and through individual action — in Ernest Poole's *His Family*."

memes.label: Regret
memes.description: A recurring emotional undercurrent in the novel — characters experience regret as the cost of their choices and the passage of time.

memes.label: "Religion"
memes.description: "The role of religious faith, Christian tradition, and spiritual questioning in the lives of the Gale family — from inherited convention to Deborah's 'religion of brotherhood' in *His Family*."

memes.label: "Remorse"
memes.description: "A recurring emotional force in His Family — remorse over past actions, lost opportunities, and the distances that grow between family members."

memes.label: Resentment
memes.description: The simmering bitterness and unspoken grievances that course through family relationships in His Family.

memes.label: "Roger Gale"
memes.description: "Protagonist of His Family — a widower approaching sixty who watches his three daughters and his city transform, struggling to hold his family together across a generation of unprecedented change in early 20th-century New York."

memes.label: "Roger Gale's Evening Walks"
memes.description: "Roger Gale's recurring evening walks through New York City — a ritual of observation and memory through which the novel registers the transformation of both the city and the man in *His Family*."

memes.label: "Roger Gale's Final Acceptance"
memes.description: "The novel's culminating event — Roger Gale's hard-won acceptance of his daughters' independence, his own mortality, and the transformed meaning of family in *His Family*."

memes.label: "Roger Gale's Illness"
memes.description: "The illness and recovery that bring Roger Gale to the hospital — the crisis that tests his family's bonds and strips away the abstractions to reveal who truly cares in *His Family*."

memes.label: "Roger Gale's Office"
memes.description: "The downtown office where Roger Gale conducts his business — a masculine domain of work, ambition, and the commercial values that define his public identity in *His Family*."

memes.label: "Roger Gale's Return to Work"
memes.description: "Roger Gale's return to his office after his prolonged illness — the event that signals his recovery and the continuity of his professional identity in *His Family*."

memes.label: Rome
memes.description: Rome is a European city referenced in "His Family" in connection with Deborah's youthful travels, Laura's art collecting, and as a symbol of the Old World's cultural and historical depth.

memes.label: Sacrifice
memes.description: The self-denial and personal cost borne by characters in *His Family*, particularly in service of family obligations and the next generation.

memes.label: Sarah
memes.description: Sarah is a maid in the Gale household in Ernest Poole's "His Family," representing the domestic servant class that supported middle-class New York family life in the early twentieth century.

memes.label: Science
memes.description: The role of scientific thinking and knowledge in His Family, from Roger's view of family as a science to the intellectual culture of the era.

memes.label: Servants
memes.description: The household servants who form the invisible infrastructure of the Gale family's domestic life and whose presence raises questions of class and economic dependency.

memes.label: Silence
memes.description: The pervasive role of silence in the Gale family's communication, often conveying more than words during moments of tension, grief, and reflection.

memes.label: "Social Class"
memes.description: "The divisions of wealth, status, and opportunity that structure the social world of *His Family* — from the immigrant tenements to the drawing rooms of Fifth Avenue."

memes.label: Success
memes.description: A central tension in the novel between material achievement and the deeper claims of family and personal fulfillment, in Ernest Poole's *His Family*.

memes.label: Suffrage
memes.description: The woman suffrage movement as a backdrop to the Gale daughters' assertion of independence and a marker of the era's redefinition of women's roles.

memes.label: Tad
memes.description: Tad is Edith Sidney's youngest child, a recurring minor character in the novel's later chapters whose cheerful presence offers moments of domestic warmth amid the family's generational transitions.

memes.label: "Tenement District"
memes.description: "The crowded immigrant neighborhoods of early 20th-century New York where Deborah Gale builds her schools — the setting that reveals the class divisions, social struggles, and reformist hopes of *His Family*."

memes.label: "The Church"
memes.description: "The church in *His Family* — a traditional institution whose moral authority and community function are quietly challenged by the novel's secular, progressive vision of social good."

memes.label: "The Club"
memes.description: "The gentlemen's club in *His Family* — an enclave of masculine sociability and class privilege where Roger Gale retreats from domestic life into a world of cigars, conversation, and male camaraderie."

memes.label: "The Dining Room"
memes.description: "The most significant room in the Gale home — where the family gathers for meals, confrontations, and the conversations that define their relationships in *His Family*."

memes.label: "The Gale Home"
memes.description: "The Gale family residence on the lower west side of New York — the physical and symbolic center of the Gale family, where Roger Gale raises three daughters, mourns his wife Judith, and watches his family transform across decades."
aliases: the house on the lower west side

memes.label: "The Great War"
memes.description: "World War I — the external catastrophe that darkens the novel's later chapters, reshaping characters' lives and confirming Roger Gale's deepest anxieties about the destructive potential of modernity in *His Family*."

memes.label: "The Hospital"
memes.description: "The setting of Roger Gale's final illness and convalescence — the place where the novel confronts mortality, care, and the bonds of family most directly in *His Family*."

memes.label: "The Park"
memes.description: "The city park in *His Family* — a patch of green in the 'tumultuous modern town' where families gather, children play, and the older New York briefly survives the city's transformation."

memes.label: Time
memes.description: The relentless passage of time in His Family, measured by the city's bells, the aging of Roger Gale, and the growth of his children and grandchildren.

memes.label: "Tradition"
memes.description: "The inherited ways of thinking, living, and valuing — rooted in 19th-century New England and old New York — that Roger Gale embodies and that his daughters variously preserve, adapt, or reject in *His Family*."

memes.label: "Urban Transformation"
memes.description: "The physical and social remaking of New York City — from a city of quiet neighborhoods to a 'tumultuous modern town' of skyscrapers, tenements, and relentless change — as witnessed by Roger Gale in *His Family*."

memes.label: War
memes.description: War — specifically the Great War — is a transformative force in "His Family," disrupting the lives of the Gale family and marking the end of the old order that Roger Gale represents.

memes.label: "Washington Square"
memes.description: "A landmark of old New York — one of the few named public spaces in *His Family* that represents the gracious, vanishing city of Roger Gale's memory, standing against the 'tumultuous modern town' rising around it."

memes.label: "Women's Roles"
memes.description: "The changing expectations, constraints, and possibilities for women in early 20th-century New York as refracted through the three Gale daughters — Edith, Laura, and Deborah — in *His Family*."

memes.label: "Work and Vocation"
memes.description: "The meaning of work — as livelihood, as identity, and as moral purpose — in the lives of Roger Gale's family, from his own business struggles to Deborah's educational calling in *His Family*."

memes.label: Youth
memes.description: Youth represents both the energy of generational change and a source of anxiety in "His Family," as the older generation — particularly Roger Gale — struggles to understand and accept the young people who will inherit the world they are remaking.
